course_title: Honours Coursework Topics in Molecular Biosciences
course_description: This one-semester course is the coursework component of the BSc(Hons) and BAdvSc(Hons) program for students undertaking an Honours year in the molecular biosciences through the School of Chemistry and Molecular Biosciences. Learning material goes beyond the content taught in first second and third level courses in these disciplines. The course will be delivered in the form of lectures, seminars, and workshops.

companions: You'll need to complete the following courses at the same time:
restrictions: Restricted to BSc(Hons) and BAdvSc(Hons) students. Permission from the School of Chemistry and Molecular Biosciences.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: This course aims to introduce the student to new and advanced topics in biochemistry, microbiology, molecular biology or genetics. The course uses several parallel streams, and students can choose the one that suits them best. In addition to acquiring new and deep understanding of concepts related to biochemistry, microbiology, molecular biology or genetics in their area of specialisation, aspects of the course content will assist the student in their research work.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply advanced knowledge and problem-solving skills in your area of specialisation (Biochemistry, Molecular Biology, Microbiology or Genetics).
Apply advanced knowledge of research techniques relevant to aspects of your research work.
Communicate advanced knowledge of research techniques relevant in your area of specialisation.

assessment_summary.title: Participation in discussion of Experimental Techniques

assessment_details.title: Journal Club Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: The oral assessment task will involve a 20-minute PowerPoint presentation on your selected paper, followed by a 20-minute question period. The slides and notes for the presentation need to be submitted as a PDF through Turnitin on the day of the presentation. The slides and notes will not be marked, but are for reference purposes only. Detailed instructions for this assessment item will be made available on Blackboard, together with a schedule for presentations. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your Slides and Notes through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extension applications must be received no later than 24 hours after the published assessment due date and time.
Read the information contained in the following links carefully before submitting an application for extension to assessment due date.
For guidance on applying for an extension, information is available here .
For the policy relating to extensions, information is available here (Part D).
Information on medical certificates
Please note the University's requirements for medical certificates here .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
This applies to cases where the presentation slides are not submitted on the day of the presentation.

assessment_details.title: Journal Club Participation
assessment_details.task_description: During your Journal Club sessions you will be assessed based on willingness to ask questions and your overall contribution to any discussion. You must read the paper to be presented prior to each session to enable you to ask relevant questions that focus on critical issues of the study, presentation or overall research theme. Students can miss 1 out of 7 journal club presentations when accompanied by a valid request with supporting documentation.

assessment_details.title: Presentation on Experimental Technique
assessment_details.task_description: You will prepare a presentation on an experimental technique of your choosing that is relevant to your Honours research project. The presentation should review the history, development, underlying mechanisms, uses, strengths and deficiencies, and future directions of your chosen technique. Presentation duration: 20 minutes, followed by 20 minutes for questions. The mark for this assessment item will consist of a 25% mark assigned by the session coordinator and a 5% peer mark collated from audience members' marks. The peer mark will be an average of the available marks, with the coordinator reserving the right to exclude extreme outliers (more than 10 marks away from the average, up or down) from the peer mark average. The slides and notes for the presentation need to be submitted as a PDF through Turnitin on the day of the presentation. The slides and notes will not be marked, but are for reference purposes only. Detailed instructions for this assessment item will be made available on Blackboard. A schedule for presentations will be made available on Blackboard at the start of the semester. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your Slides and Notes through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1

assessment_details.title: Participation in discussion of Experimental Techniques
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: During your presentations of experimental techniques you will be assessed based on willingness to ask questions and your overall contribution to any discussion. You should look into basic aspects of the method prior to each session to enable you to ask relevant questions that focus on critical aspects of this technique, presentation or overall relevance to the research topic this method is to be applied in. As part of your participation mark, you will be required to complete peer-marking evaluations of the presentations, which will count 5% towards the presenter's presentation mark (see above - marks for presentations) Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
During each session that you attend, you will complete a peer-marking form for the presentations that are given in the session.
The forms will focus on the quality of the presentation, the slides used and answers to questions.
All students should complete the forms before leaving the session.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This activity must be completed in person during class and involves oral participation, such as asking questions, as well as completing a peer-evaluation of the presentations given that day.
Students can miss 1 out of 6 method presentations when accompanied by a valid request with supporting documentation.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life.
